fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Patio Area; Perimeter Fencing
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Limited Rear Access; Dense Vegetation
fr.recommendations: Enhancing response effectiveness to this property could be achieved by addressing several key areas. Improving visibility and maneuverability around the main building and landscaped areas may support efficient operations. Creating additional access paths or enhancing existing routes, particularly to provide rear access, may facilitate more effective emergency responder movement throughout the property. Managing the dense vegetation could enhance visibility and accessibility, improving navigation and operational efficiency. Additional or clearer signage identifying access points and areas for staging could assist responders in quickly orienting themselves upon arrival. Improving entry and exit flow for the single access point could also aid in a smoother and more organized response.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Tree Cluster; Surrounding Open Space; Continuous Shrub Cover
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing the separation between vegetation and structures could improve the property's wildfire resilience. Managing the vegetation adjacent to structures and access routes by creating a buffer of non-combustible space can reduce exposure to fire. Addressing the dense shrub cover by thinning and breaking up fuel continuity may prevent the rapid spread of fire. Maintaining open spaces and extending them around the property, particularly where tree clusters are present, can further minimize opportunities for fire spread. Continued management of the vegetated perimeter by selectively removing overgrown areas would help to maintain a buffer of reduced fuel continuity. Implementing these strategies may support firefighter access and suppression efforts, promoting a more prepared and wildfire-resilient property.
